engineGuide.h2: Mitsubishi Eclipse Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mitsubishi Eclipse across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Petrol
entries.history: Iconic turbo engine powering GSX/GST; tuner favourite but crank walk risk on high-mileage units.
compatibleVariants: GS, GST, GSX, RS Turbo, Spyder GST
commonFailures: Crankshaft thrust bearing wear (crank walk) 80k-120k miles
Turbo oil seal failure on high-boost applications
Timing belt snap if not replaced every 60k miles
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G63 Mitsubishi Eclipse engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Turbo Petrol
entries.history: Factory-turbo variant of 4G63; delivers 210hp but demands meticulous maintenance to avoid catastrophic failure.
commonFailures: Crank walk due to thin thrust bearings on 2G block
Turbocharger bearing wear above 15psi boost
Oil pump pickup screen clogging causing starvation
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G63T Mitsubishi Eclipse engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: Reliable non-turbo workhorse; balance shaft bearing failures can cause knock if oil changes neglected.
compatibleVariants: RS, GS, Spyder GS, Eclipse (1G/2G/3G base)
commonFailures: Balance shaft bearing seizure causing bottom-end knock
Oil consumption from worn piston rings on high-mileage units
Head gasket failure if overheated
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G64 Mitsubishi Eclipse engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L MIVEC Petrol
entries.history: Final evolution of 4G family; MIVEC variable valve timing improved efficiency but retained balance shaft design.
commonFailures: Balance shaft bearing wear similar to 4G64
MIVEC solenoid clogging causing rough idle
Timing chain tensioner wear on late models
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G69 Mitsubishi Eclipse engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L V6 Petrol
entries.history: Smooth V6 powering GTS models; interference design means timing belt failure causes valve damage.
commonFailures: Timing belt snap causing bent valves if not replaced at 60k miles
Oil pump drive belt failure leading to oil starvation
Intake manifold gasket leaks causing rough running
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6G72 Mitsubishi Eclipse engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L Turbo Petrol (Eclipse Cross)
entries.history: Modern downsized turbo; piston ring design flaw on 2018-2019 models caused excessive oil consumption.
compatibleVariants: Eclipse Cross 1.5, Eclipse Cross 1.5T, Eclipse Cross PHEV (petrol unit)
commonFailures: Piston ring wear causing oil consumption on 2018-2019 builds
Turbocharger wastegate sticking causing boost issues
Carbon buildup on intake valves from direct injection
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4B40 Mitsubishi Eclipse engine replacement

entries.title: 2.2L DI-D Diesel
entries.history: Modern clean diesel for Eclipse Cross; complex emissions systems increase failure points but deliver strong torque.
commonFailures: High-pressure injector failure causing misfire and DPF clogging
Timing chain stretch on high-mileage units above 100k miles
EGR cooler cracking leading to coolant loss
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4N15 Mitsubishi Eclipse engine replacement

entries.title: 1.5L PHEV Hybrid
entries.history: Plug-in hybrid pairing 4B40 petrol with twin electric motors; battery health critical for replacement decisions.
commonFailures: Traction battery degradation reducing electric-only range
Inverter coolant pump failure causing hybrid system faults
Petrol unit oil consumption as per standard 4B40
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4B40+EM Mitsubishi Eclipse engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Eclipse Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Eclipse spans iconic turbo petrols and modern efficient units, but key failure patterns drive replacement demand. The 4G63 turbo's crank walk, 6G72 V6 timing belt risks, and Eclipse Cross 4B40 oil consumption dominate UK enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the cost comparisons below to decide whether repair or replacement offers better value for your Eclipse.

problems.h4: 4G63 Crank Walk Bearing Failure - Why 2G Eclipse Turbo Engines Fail
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Eclipse GST, GSX, Spyder GST (1995-1999, 4G63T)
problems.rootCause: Thin factory thrust bearings wear under high load, allowing crankshaft end-play that destroys the block.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Oil pan drop, bearing clearance check, oil pump screen clean
repairOptions.longevity: Only viable before knock appears; catches early wear

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded thrust bearings, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution; upgraded components prevent repeat failure

problems.recommendation: On a 2G Eclipse valued under £3,000, replacement with a rebuilt 4G63T featuring upgraded bearings offers better long-term value than chasing a preventative fix on a worn block. We recommend replacement once any knock is detected.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G63T engine prices for your Mitsubishi Eclipse

problems.h4: Eclipse Cross 4B40 Oil Consumption - Piston Ring Design Flaw
problems.rootCause: Early-production piston rings allow excessive oil passage into combustion chamber, causing burning and carbon buildup.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine strip, new rings fitted, cylinder hone, reassemble
repairOptions.longevity: Addresses root cause but labour-intensive; success depends on bore condition

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt unit with revised ring design, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Most reliable solution; avoids risk of bore damage during ring job

problems.recommendation: For 2018-2019 Eclipse Cross models showing oil consumption, a rebuilt 4B40 with revised rings offers peace of mind and warranty protection. If caught very early (<40k miles), ring replacement may be viable but carries bore-scoring risk.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 4B40 Eclipse Cross engines

problems.h4: 6G72 V6 Timing Belt Failure - Catastrophic Interference Damage
problems.rootCause: Rubber timing belt deteriorates with age; snap causes pistons to strike open valves in this interference engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Timing belt, tensioner, water pump replacement per 60k-mile schedule
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents catastrophic failure; essential maintenance item

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt V6 with new belts fitted, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option after belt snap causes valve/piston damage

problems.recommendation: If your 6G72 belt is overdue, replace it immediately-cost is minor versus engine destruction. If the belt has already snapped, replacement with a rebuilt 6G72 is the only viable path; repair is rarely economical.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 6G72 V6 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Eclipse

problems.h4: 4N15 Diesel Injector & DPF Failure - Eclipse Cross 2.2 DI-D
problems.rootCause: High-pressure injectors leak or clog, causing incomplete combustion that rapidly clogs the DPF and triggers limp mode.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove/replace faulty injectors, DPF off-car clean or replace, ECU reset
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if block is sound; success depends on early diagnosis

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt diesel with new injectors fitted, emissions systems checked, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when multiple components failed or block damaged

problems.recommendation: For Eclipse Cross diesels with injector-related DPF clogging, get a specialist diagnosis first. If only one injector failed and the block is healthy, repair may suffice. If multiple injectors or turbo are affected, replacement offers better warranty protection.
problems.cta: Find rebuilt 4N15 Eclipse Cross diesel engine prices

problems.h4: 4G64/4G69 Balance Shaft Bearing Seizure - Bottom-End Knock
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Eclipse RS, GS, GT, Spyder (1989-2012, 4G64/4G69)
problems.rootCause: Balance shaft bearings wear from oil starvation or infrequent changes, causing seizure and catastrophic knock.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full synthetic oil change, filter replacement, oil pressure check
repairOptions.longevity: Prevents wear; essential maintenance but won't fix existing knock

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded balance shaft bearings, bench tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only solution once knock appears; upgraded bearings prevent recurrence

problems.recommendation: Once a bottom-end knock is audible on a 4G64/4G69, replacement is the only reliable path. Preventative oil changes every 5k miles with quality synthetic are critical to avoid this failure.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 4G64/4G69 Mitsubishi Eclipse engines

problems.group: Eclipse Cross CVT Stress Leading to Engine Strain
problems.h4: Eclipse Cross CVT Stress Leading to Engine Strain
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Eclipse Cross 1.5T, 2.2 DI-D (2018-2023, CVT models)
problems.rootCause: CVT belt slip or valve body failure causes erratic load on engine, accelerating wear on turbo, bearings, and mounts.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: CVT fluid change, valve body repair or replacement, adaptation reset
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine is healthy; addresses root cause of erratic load

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine plus reconditioned CVT, full system test, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value when both powertrain components show wear

problems.recommendation: If your Eclipse Cross shows CVT judder alongside engine symptoms, get both systems diagnosed. Replacing only the engine without addressing CVT issues risks repeat failure. A combined engine+CVT package often delivers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Compare Eclipse Cross engine and CVT replacement packages

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Eclipse
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're dealing with 4G63 crank walk, 4B40 oil consumption, or 6G72 timing belt damage, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Eclipse specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Eclipse Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Eclipse and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

items.title: Mitsubishi Eclipse 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.8L 4G37 powered early RS and base GS models (1989-1994), offering reliable entry-level performance but prone to head gasket issues if overheated.

items.title: Mitsubishi Eclipse 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The versatile 2.0L family spans naturally aspirated 4G63 and turbocharged 4G63T units, powering everything from base GS to iconic GSX AWD models across 1G and 2G generations.

items.title: Mitsubishi Eclipse 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.4L 4G64 and MIVEC-equipped 4G69 powered later GS, GT and Spyder models, offering improved torque but retaining balance shaft designs requiring diligent oil maintenance.

items.title: Mitsubishi Eclipse 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The smooth 3.0L 6G72 V6 powered GTS and Spyder GTS models (2000-2005), delivering refined power but requiring strict timing belt replacement intervals to avoid catastrophic interference damage.
commonFailurePoints: Timing belt snap causing valve damage
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Eclipse 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mitsubishi Eclipse 1.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: The modern 1.5L turbo 4B40 powers Eclipse Cross models (2018-2023), delivering strong efficiency but suffering piston ring design flaws on early 2018-2019 builds causing oil consumption.

items.title: Mitsubishi Eclipse 2.2L Diesel Engines

items.description: Diesel options are limited to the Eclipse Cross 2.2 DI-D (2018-2021), offering strong torque for towing but complex emissions systems requiring specialist attention during replacement.
items.descriptor: Diesel options are limited to the Eclipse Cross 2.2 DI-D (2018-2021), offering strong torque for towing but complex emissions systems requiring specialist attention during replacement.

families: 4N15 - modern 2.2L DI-D; injector and DPF failures drive replacement demand

High-pressure injector failures above 70k miles
DPF clogging from short-journey driving patterns

items.title: Mitsubishi Eclipse Plug-in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: The Eclipse Cross PHEV pairs a 1.5L turbo petrol with twin electric motors and a traction battery, requiring specialist assessment of both combustion and electrical systems during replacement.
items.descriptor: The Eclipse Cross PHEV pairs a 1.5L turbo petrol with twin electric motors and a traction battery, requiring specialist assessment of both combustion and electrical systems during replacement.
families: 4B40+EM - 1.5L turbo petrol plus electric motor system (Eclipse Cross PHEV)
Petrol engine drives front wheels; twin electric motors provide AWD and EV-only capability
Traction battery offers ~24 miles electric-only range on full charge
Engine replacement requires hybrid system inspection and battery health verification
importantNotes: Petrol unit shares 4B40 oil consumption risks with standard Eclipse Cross
Traction battery degradation reduces EV range but doesn't prevent petrol operation
Specialist suppliers required; availability more limited than standard engines

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Eclipse Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mitsubishi Eclipse production run, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The original Eclipse launched with Mitsubishi's proven 4G family, establishing the tuner-friendly reputation that would define the model.
keyChanges: First-generation Eclipse introduced with FWD platform
Turbo option (4G63T) launched in 1990 for GST/GSX
knownFor: Lightweight chassis ideal for modification
4G63T becomes tuner icon but crank walk risk emerges
Simple mechanics favour DIY maintenance
Early models prone to rust in UK climate
years.cta: Get quotes for 1989-1994 Mitsubishi Eclipse replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Eclipse 1990 engine replacement • 4G63T turbo rebuild cost • 1G Eclipse crank walk fix

years.description: The second generation refined the formula with updated styling and the infamous 2G 4G63T block that would dominate enthusiast replacement demand.
keyChanges: New body style with improved aerodynamics
2G 4G63T block introduced with thinner thrust bearings
AWD GSX discontinued after 1996 in some markets
knownFor: 4G63T crank walk failure becomes widespread above 80k miles
Peak era for tuner modifications and aftermarket support
Spyder convertible adds weight affecting handling
Oil pump pickup screen clogging causes starvation failures
years.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G63T Mitsubishi Eclipse engine prices
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Eclipse 1997 engine replacement • 4G63T crank walk repair • GST turbo engine rebuild cost

years.description: The third generation shifted focus to refinement with the smooth 6G72 V6, but interference design made timing belt maintenance critical.
keyChanges: New platform with longer wheelbase and heavier weight
6G72 3.0L V6 becomes flagship engine for GTS models
Turbo options discontinued; focus on NA refinement
knownFor: 6G72 timing belt failure causes catastrophic valve damage
Heavier chassis reduces tuner appeal vs 1G/2G
Improved interior quality and daily usability
Balance shaft issues persist on 4G64 units
years.cta: Get quotes for 2000-2005 Mitsubishi Eclipse replacement engines
years.ticker: Mitsubishi Eclipse 2003 engine replacement • 6G72 timing belt replacement cost • GTS V6 engine rebuild

years.description: The fourth generation brought the refined 4G69 MIVEC engine but marked the end of the Eclipse sports coupe line in most markets.
keyChanges: Final evolution of Eclipse coupe platform
4G69 MIVEC replaces 4G64 with variable valve timing
Production ended for most markets after 2012
knownFor: 4G69 retains balance shaft design requiring oil vigilance
Improved fuel economy vs earlier 2.4L units
MIVEC solenoid clogging can cause rough idle
Final chapter for iconic Eclipse coupe nameplate
years.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G69 Mitsubishi Eclipse engine prices

years.description: The Eclipse name returned as a compact crossover, with modern downsized turbo petrol and clean diesel options bringing new failure patterns.
keyChanges: Eclipse name applied to new compact SUV platform
knownFor: 4B40 piston ring oil consumption on 2018-2019 builds
4N15 injector and DPF failures drive diesel replacement demand
CVT reliability concerns adding stress to engine components
Modern emissions systems increase complexity and repair costs
years.cta: Get quotes for 2018-2021 Mitsubishi Eclipse Cross replacement engines
years.ticker: Eclipse Cross 2019 oil consumption fix • 4B40 piston ring replacement • 4N15 injector failure cost

years.description: Plug-in hybrid technology arrived alongside a facelift, with revised 4B40 addressing early oil consumption but hybrid complexity adding new considerations.
keyChanges: Eclipse Cross PHEV launched with 4B40+EM hybrid system
Facelift models received revised piston rings on 4B40
knownFor: Revised 4B40 rings reduce oil consumption on post-2020 builds
PHEV traction battery health critical for replacement decisions
Hybrid system requires specialist assessment during engine replacement
Strong residual values support investment in quality rebuilt units
years.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Eclipse Cross PHEV engine availability
years.ticker: Eclipse Cross PHEV battery replacement • 4B40 revised piston rings • Eclipse Cross 2022 engine availability

engineYears.closing: Not sure which year your Mitsubishi Eclipse was built? Enter your registration number and we'll identify the exact engine, model year, and compatible replacement options instantly - all backed by a minimum 12‑month warranty from UK Mitsubishi Eclipse specialists.

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Eclipse Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Eclipse engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: Rebuilt Mitsubishi Eclipse engine prices range from £900 for early 1.8L units to £4,500 for 4G63T turbo or Eclipse Cross PHEV systems, depending on code, variant and condition type.
keyPoints: 4G63T turbo rebuilds typically £1,800-£4,500 supply only
Standard petrol engines (4G64/4G69) £1,200-£2,800 rebuilt
Eclipse Cross 4B40/4N15 units £1,500-£4,200 depending on fuel type
Supply & fit labour adds £450-£1,200 to engine price
All EngineMarket quotes include minimum 12-month warranty
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Mitsubishi Eclipse engine prices for your variant

items.question: Is the 4G63 turbo engine in the Eclipse GSX reliable?
items.answer: The 4G63T is robust when maintained but suffers crank walk bearing failure on 2G blocks (1995-1999) above 80k miles if oil changes are neglected or boost is excessive.
keyPoints: Crank walk caused by thin factory thrust bearings wearing under load
Regular synthetic oil changes every 3-5k miles critical for longevity
Rebuilt units with upgraded bearings eliminate repeat failure risk
Turbo components require separate inspection during replacement
Strong aftermarket support aids maintenance and upgrades
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt 4G63T Mitsubishi Eclipse GSX engines

items.question: What causes oil consumption on the Eclipse Cross 1.5T engine?
items.answer: Early 2018-2019 Eclipse Cross 4B40 engines suffer piston ring design flaws allowing oil into the combustion chamber; revised rings on post-2020 builds largely resolve the issue.
keyPoints: Faulty rings permit excessive oil passage causing burning and carbon buildup
Symptoms include blue exhaust smoke, frequent top-ups, fouled spark plugs
Ring replacement possible but labour-intensive; rebuilt engine often better value
Mitsubishi issued technical service bulletins but no formal recall in UK
Use full synthetic 0W-20 oil and monitor levels closely on affected models
items.cta: Find rebuilt 4B40 Eclipse Cross engine prices with revised rings

items.question: Should I repair or replace a failed 6G72 V6 in my Eclipse GTS?
items.answer: If the 6G72 timing belt snapped causing valve damage, replacement with a rebuilt unit is almost always more economical than attempting cylinder head repairs on this interference engine.
keyPoints: 6G72 is interference design; belt snap bends valves and can damage pistons
Head repair plus valve job often exceeds £2,500 with no warranty
Rebuilt 6G72 engines £1,400-£3,200 with 12-month warranty included
Factor in labour savings when choosing supply & fit rebuilt option
Preventative belt replacement every 60k miles avoids catastrophic failure
items.cta: Compare rebuilt 6G72 V6 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Eclipse GTS

items.question: Are used Mitsubishi Eclipse engines a good budget option?
items.answer: Used engines can be cost-effective for project cars or low-value Eclipses, but always request compression test results and mileage verification before purchase.
keyPoints: Used 4G63/4G64 units often £600-£1,800 vs £1,200+ for rebuilt
Risk of hidden wear or imminent failure not visible on inspection
Warranty typically limited to 1-3 months vs 12 months on rebuilt
Ideal for short-term fixes, track builds, or cars valued under £2,000
Always verify engine code matches your vehicle's DVLA record
items.cta: Check used Mitsubishi Eclipse engine availability with compression reports

items.question: How long does a Mitsubishi Eclipse engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only engines typically dispatch in 3-7 days; supply & fit installations require 1-2 additional days at a specialist garage, depending on parts availability and workshop schedule.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engines often built to order; allow 5-10 days lead time
Used engines may be available for next-day dispatch if in stock
Supply & fit labour adds 1-2 days for removal, fitting and run-in
Complex variants (GSX AWD, PHEV) may require longer specialist scheduling
EngineMarket suppliers provide realistic timelines when quoting
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Eclipse engine supply & fit with delivery estimates

items.question: Will a rebuilt Mitsubishi Eclipse engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes-every engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covering parts and labour for manufacturing defects.
keyPoints: Warranty valid UK-wide at any participating specialist garage
Covers internal components, gaskets, seals and machining defects
Excludes damage from misuse, incorrect installation or lack of maintenance
Keep service records and use recommended oil to maintain coverage
Confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Eclipse rebuilt engine warranty terms across suppliers

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Mitsubishi Eclipse?
items.answer: Engine swaps are possible but require significant modification to wiring, ECU and mounts; for most owners, replacing with the original code offers the most reliable, warranty-backed solution.
keyPoints: 4G63T into non-turbo Eclipse requires turbo plumbing, ECU and fuel upgrades
Eclipse Cross engines not compatible with older coupe platforms
DVLA may require notification and potential re-testing for major changes
Specialist knowledge essential; DIY swaps risk reliability and insurance validity
EngineMarket specialists can advise on feasible swap options if requested
items.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Eclipse engine compatibility with our specialists

items.question: What's the most reliable Mitsubishi Eclipse engine to buy?
items.answer: The 4G69 2.4L MIVEC (2006-2012) and revised post-2020 4B40 1.5T offer the best balance of modern efficiency and proven reliability when maintained properly.
keyPoints: 4G69 benefits from MIVEC efficiency and late-production refinements
Post-2020 4B40 units feature revised piston rings addressing oil consumption
Avoid early 2G 4G63T blocks (1995-1999) unless rebuilt with upgraded bearings
Regular oil changes with quality synthetic are critical for any Eclipse engine
Service history matters more than mileage for long-term reliability
items.cta: Compare prices for reliable Mitsubishi Eclipse engine options

items.question: How do I know which engine code my Mitsubishi Eclipse has?
items.answer: Enter your registration number above for instant DVLA verification, or locate the engine code stamped on the block near the oil filter housing or on the vehicle's VIN plate.
keyPoints: DVLA lookup via EngineMarket instantly confirms code, fuel type and variant
Physical codes: 4G63/4G64 near oil filter; 6G72 on timing cover; 4B40 on valve cover
Owner's manual and service records often list the factory-fitted code
Eclipse Cross codes: 4B40 (petrol), 4N15 (diesel), 4B40+EM (PHEV)
Matching the exact code ensures compatibility and warranty validity
items.cta: Identify your Mitsubishi Eclipse engine code instantly with our registration tool
